title: Launch first-story onboarding flow with guided chapter and scene scaffold
description:
## Problem
New users face a blank-page experience and often fail to reach a meaningful first writing milestone. Without guided setup, activation and early retention suffer.

## Target user
First-time users evaluating the app in their first session, especially writers migrating from generic docs tools.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase activation rate: % of new users who create first story and first scene in one session
- Increase day-1 to day-7 retention for users who complete onboarding
- Reduce first-session drop-off before first 200 words

## Scope
- Add an optional onboarding flow after first story creation with 3 guided steps:
  1. Name story + genre intent
  2. Create first chapter and first scene
  3. Set a lightweight first-session objective (draft/revise/brainstorm)
- Pre-fill the editor with a minimal scaffold (chapter title + scene prompt placeholder)
- Provide skip and dismiss controls so experienced users can bypass onboarding
- Track onboarding completion state per user to avoid repeated prompts

## Non-goals
- Full interactive tutorial for every feature
- Mandatory onboarding gates that block editor access
- Personalized AI coaching in onboarding v1

## Acceptance criteria
- New user is offered onboarding once after first story creation
- User can complete onboarding in under 2 minutes and land in editable first scene
- User can skip onboarding at any step and still access core editor
- Onboarding completion/skip state persists and prevents repeated prompting
- Event instrumentation exists for each onboarding step and completion outcome

## Priority rationale
The app already has strong drafting/planning surface area; onboarding is the missing bridge from sign-up intent to actual writing behavior. Shipping this now should improve activation before adding more advanced features.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research
